name: NLC India
full_name: NLC India Limited (formerly Neyveli Lignite Corporation)

identification.ownership_details: Schedule A Central PSE with Navratna status; GoI institutions hold additional 4.31%

coal.notes: JV coal plants: NTPL Tuticorin 1000 MW + NUPPL Ghatampur 660 MW

lignite.notes: Neyveli TPS-I Exp 420, TPS-II 1470, TPS-II Exp 500, NNTPS 1000, Barsingsar 250

solar.notes: Tamil Nadu, A&N Islands (20 MW with 8 MWh BESS), Neyveli captive

wind.notes: Kaluneerkulam, Tenkasi District - 34 WTGs x 1.5 MW

capacity.under_construction_details: NUPPL Units 2-3 (1320 MW), Talabira TPP (2400 MW), TPS-II 2nd Exp (1000 MW)
capacity.pipeline_re_details: Khavda 600 MW, Barsingsar Solar 1110 MW, CPSU Solar 510 MW, SECI Hybrid 150 MW, Telangana 200 MW, Assam JV 1000 MW
capacity.source: NLC India Annual Report FY2023-24, NSE filings

generation.notes: 27,865.58 MU consolidated FY25; +2.8% YoY from 27,106 MU; Thermal 92%, RE 8%

plf_highlights.nntps_notes: NNTPS (2x500 MW) single-month peak (March 2024), NOT annual company-wide figure
plf_highlights.company_wide_annual_plf_notes: Estimated annual company-wide PLF based on generation (25,765 GWh thermal) / capacity (~5,536 MW thermal) / 8760h = ~53%. CEA all-India thermal average: 69%. NLC India historical company-wide PLF approximately 55-70% depending on year.
plf_highlights.notes: WARNING: Previous use of 98% CF for NLC India was a single unit's best month. Annual company-wide figure is approximately 55-70%.

interest_expense.notes: INR 932 crore FY25; +9.8% YoY from 849 Cr; Q4 FY25 highest at 325 Cr
interest_expense.source: NLC India Consolidated Financial Statements FY25

current_assets.notes: INR 8,065 crore FY25 (Cash 187 + Receivables 4,122 + Inventory 1,913 + Other 1,843); down from 9,326 Cr FY24
current_assets.source: NLC India Consolidated Balance Sheet FY25

current_liabilities.notes: INR 10,707 crore FY25 (ST Debt 2,628 + Payables 1,898 + Current LT Debt 1,274 + Other 4,907); up from 7,947 Cr FY24
current_liabilities.source: NLC India Consolidated Balance Sheet FY25

operating_cash_flow.notes: INR 8,977 crore FY25; +62.9% YoY from 5,512 Cr; improved receivables collection (113 days vs 239 days)
operating_cash_flow.source: NLC India Consolidated Cash Flow Statement FY25

capex.notes: INR 7,700+ crore FY25 - highest-ever investment year; RE commitment INR 50,000 crore through 2030

credit_rating.notes: CRISIL AAA (NCDs 3,000 Cr, Bank facilities 6,659 Cr); ICRA AAA (NCDs 2,000 Cr)

scope1.notes: Very high due to lignite combustion; -11.2% YoY from 28.52 MtCO2

intensity.notes: Estimated ~1.0 tCO2/MWh - exceeds India grid average (0.716 tCO2/MWh) due to lignite

net_zero.notes: No company-specific target; aligned with India COP26 commitment

sbti.notes: In process of setting targets; not yet submitted for validation

coal_policy.coal_exit_details: No phase-out timeline; 4.7 GW thermal under construction

re_targets.notes: Aggressive RE expansion but continued thermal additions through 2030

capex_allocation.notes: Clean vs thermal capex split not explicitly disclosed

clean_energy_initiatives.notes: Diversification into green hydrogen and methanol

coal_plants.notes: Heavy lignite/coal focus with 4.7 GW thermal under construction

esg_ratings.sustainalytics_notes: High exposure due to lignite combustion carbon intensity

interest_coverage.notes: EBITDA 6,513 Cr / Interest 932 Cr = 7.0x; CRISIL adjusted 5.30x FY24

ffo_to_debt_pct.notes: OCF 8,977 Cr / Debt 22,429 Cr = 40.0%; +63% YoY cash generation

current_ratio.notes: CA 8,065 Cr / CL 10,707 Cr = 0.75x; down from 1.17x FY24 due to capex-related ST borrowings

debt_to_capital_pct.notes: Debt 22,429 Cr / (Debt + Equity 39,043 Cr) = 57.4%; D/E 1.17x well within CERC norm of 2.33x

credit_metrics_b5.notes: 4/5 metrics pass. Only current ratio (0.75x) fails due to highest-ever capex year (₹7,700+ Cr). Interest coverage 7.0x and FFO/Debt 40.0% exceptionally strong. AAA ratings from CRISIL/ICRA/CARE confirmed. ₹3,500 Cr undrawn bank lines mitigate liquidity squeeze.
credit_metrics_b5.source: Calculated from NLC India FY25 Consolidated Financial Statements, CARE Jan 2026

data_resolved.source: NLC India Consolidated Financial Statements FY25
NLC India Consolidated Cash Flow Statement FY25
NLC India Consolidated Balance Sheet FY25
NLC India Consolidated Balance Sheet FY25

data_sources.rating_reports: CRISIL Rating Rationale April 2024, ICRA February 2025

verification_checklist.coal_capacity_note: 5,300 MW thermal (3,640 MW lignite + 1,660 MW coal JVs) = 78.7%
verification_checklist.net_zero_note: 2070 - aligned with India national target, no company-specific commitment
verification_checklist.b5_data_note: All B5 data complete; 4/5 metrics pass

analyst_notes: NLC India is a lignite-focused Navratna PSU with 78.7% thermal capacity (3,640 MW lignite + 1,660 MW coal JVs). Despite AAA ratings and strong cash generation (OCF +63% YoY to ₹8,977 Cr), company faces significant transition risk: (1) Very high Scope 1 emissions (25.15 MtCO2); (2) Emissions intensity ~1.0 tCO2/MWh exceeds grid average; (3) 4.7 GW additional thermal under construction; (4) No coal/lignite phase-out timeline; (5) Sustainalytics 'Severe' ESG risk rating (44.8). FY25 financials show exceptional operational performance: highest-ever EBITDA (₹6,513 Cr), interest coverage 7.0x, FFO/Debt 40.0%. Only weakness is current ratio (0.75x) due to intensive capex (₹7,700+ Cr). ETCB outcomes: B1 FAIL (78.7% thermal at-risk), B2 FAIL (CCE 28.6% at medium carbon price), B3 PASS (no LCOE crossover), B4 FAIL (2070 net-zero, no SBTi, only 21.3% RE), B5 PASS (4/5 metrics). Final score: 2/5 Low-medium tier. Interesting paradox: lignite-heavy NLC passes B5 with strong financials while pure-RE AGEL fails B5 due to growth financing.
